name: philosophy_axes
description: 5-axis philosophy filter for Trend Harvester - based on Hugh Kim's Loopy-Era approach

practical.name: Practicality
practical.description: Does this trend solve real problems for中小型 model users?
questions: Does it work on small models (MiniMax-M2.7 class)?
Does it reduce cost or increase efficiency?
Is it implementable without Claude/GPT-4 class models?
Does it help with the 3 constraints: context window, reliability, cost?

integratable.name: Integratability
integratable.description: Can this trend be integrated into Drewgent's P-structure?
questions: Does it map to P0-P6 structure?
Does it enhance existing systems (hooks, orchestrator, memory)?
Is the integration complexity reasonable?
Does it conflict with existing Drewgent architecture?

drewgent_first.name: Drewgent-First
drewgent_first.description: Does this prioritize Drewgent's specific use case (Korean,中小型 model, CLI)?
questions: Is it relevant to Korean-language users?
Does it work in CLI environment?
Does it respect Drewgent's brain-based P-structure?
Does it help中小型 model performance?

no_model_dependency.name: Model-Independent
no_model_dependency.description: Does this NOT depend on switching to larger models?
questions: Does this require Claude/GPT-4? → REJECT
Does this suggest using smaller/faster models? → PRIORITIZE
Is this about model-agnostic techniques?
Does this help MiniMax-M2.7 perform better?

safety.name: Safety & Trust
safety.description: Does this maintain or improve system safety and reliability?
questions: Does it introduce security risks?
Does it improve reliability?
Does it enhance user trust?
Does it align with Drewgent's HARD hooks principles?
